---
title: "nitrix"
subtitle: "GPU-accelerated pure numerics for neuroimaging in JAX/XLA"
toc: false
---

`nitrix` is the substrate numerics layer of the federation: statistics, geometry,
registration, signal, graph, and morphology, written as pure JAX/XLA so they run —
differentiably, batched, JIT-compiled — on a hardware accelerator.

The documentation follows the [Diátaxis](https://diataxis.fr) four-mode structure —
**tutorials** to learn, **how-to guides** to get a task done, **reference** to look
things up, and **explanation** to understand why. The explanation layer is complete;
the others are taking shape. New here? Start with
[the primer](explanation/primer/index.qmd).

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Tutorials

*Learning-oriented — guided lessons that take a newcomer from zero to a first
working result.*

**Planned.** In the meantime, every explanation chapter's figures are runnable
end-to-end, and [the primer](explanation/primer/index.qmd) is the gentlest on-ramp
to the library's foundations.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### How-to guides

*Task-oriented — direct recipes for a specific job ("register an EPI to a T1", "fit
a mass-univariate GLM across a brain").*

**Planned.** The recipe-style chapters come closest today — e.g. the
[registration recipes](explanation/registration/recipes.qmd) (which mover for which
problem) and each unit's performance chapter.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Explanation

*Understanding-oriented — derive the mathematics, justify the design, point at the
code.* The textbook layer, and the most complete part of the docs.

- [The primer](explanation/primer/index.qmd) — programming the GPU, the JAX/XLA
  compiler, the calculus of automatic differentiation, and inductive bias
  *(start here)*
- [Statistical modelling](explanation/statistics/index.qmd) — the model ladder
  (GLM → LME → GAM → GLMM → GAMM → HSGP) and the engine beneath it
- [Discrete geometry](explanation/geometry/index.qmd) — meshes, volumes, and the
  operators on them, over a shared semiring substrate
- [Registration](explanation/registration/index.qmd) — maps between geometries: the
  transform ladder, similarity, resampling, and the optimisation that finds the map

More units (signal, connectivity and labels) will follow the same pattern.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Reference

*Information-oriented — precise, complete API documentation for every public
function and type.*

**In progress, not yet published.** Auto-generated signature stubs currently cover
`nitrix.stats`; the rest of the public surface, and the authored prose that turns a
signature into usable documentation, are forthcoming.
:::

:::
